formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Haptens are small, nonproteincmolecules without intrinsic immunogenicity; they must bind to carrier proteins to elicit an immune response. This explains why certain drugs cause allergic reactions after metabolic conversion into reactive intermediates that form complexes with self-proteins—leading clinically significant Type IV hypersensitivity responses.

Final Answer:

\boxed{\text{C}}

The summary focuses solely on core clinical relevance regarding haptens' role in triggering adverse drug reactions via formation of neoantigens through covalent modification of host proteins during pharmacological exposure.

Only include this final answer line – nothing else.

\boxed{\text{C}}. Answer choice: C
